page.title: LPFS Command Center
page.subtitle: Operator and strategy-improvement snapshot for LPFS. Static, additive, evidence-linked, and not broker truth by itself.
page.freshness_warning: This page is a generated static dashboard. Before any live operation, deployment, restart, broker action, or strategy change, capture fresh dual-VPS status and broker evidence.

repo_context.runtime_sha_note: The latest deployed robustness/runtime boundary before hold was 6c4ecb1. Both runners are now stopped under kill switch; local tracked docs may be ahead of deployed runtime because docs/process commits do not require runner restart.

operating_boundary.summary: Both LPFS lanes are HELD/FLAT after the 2026-07-09 operator-approved flatten. Tasks are disabled, kill switches are active, and broker-authoritative LPFS pending orders and active positions are 0 on both lanes.
operating_boundary.truth_boundary: Current hold packet evidence only. Final status is AMBIGUOUS by design because runtime state and journals were not rewritten after manual broker flatten, creating an expected state/broker mismatch. Do not resume or reconcile without a separate reviewed plan.

strategy_context.active_strategy: LPFS baseline: V13 mechanics + V15 risk buckets + V22 LP/FS separation
strategy_context.current_decision: No live strategy change or resumption is approved; LPFS is flat and paused for planning.
strategy_context.what_is_being_tested: Offline only: whether repeated weak cohorts, spread/commission drag, H8 compressed risk, and lane divergence explain forward underperformance well enough to justify a future reversible proposal.
strategy_context.next_responsible_action: Review flattened live evidence, isolate spread/execution and cohort drivers, then produce a no-live-change research plan before any resumption or filter proposal.
not_approved: No runner/task restart while project is on hold
No dashboard-driven live operation without fresh status evidence

weekly_evidence.freshness_note: The weekly packet is ignored local evidence. The stable docs/live_weekly_performance.html page may lag unless intentionally regenerated.

lanes.working: Some H4 and isolated winners remained positive, but not enough to offset concentrated losses.
lanes.weak: H8 had 4 closed trades, 0 wins, about -4.62R. Long side was about -4.09R. EURCHF, GBPNZD, and AUDNZD were concentrated loss contributors.

lanes.working: Roughly flat strategy R and slightly positive broker PnL. H4 was positive.
lanes.weak: H12 and H8 were negative, and account outcome diverged from strategy R. Treat this as attribution evidence, not a strategy failure by itself.

lanes.working: IC did not confirm the full FTMO damage. Cross-lane comparison is still useful.
lanes.weak: Combined week is weak because FTMO underperformed materially.

active_candidates.name: H8 compressed risk
active_candidates.current_hypothesis: H8 trades with compressed risk, especially low-spread intersections, may be a weak subset, but prior simple low-spread-only filtering was rejected.
active_candidates.next_test: Build offline cohort matrix across FTMO and IC using recent 3/6/12 month windows plus long-history guardrails.

active_candidates.name: FTMO H8/long and symbol loss concentration
active_candidates.current_hypothesis: The latest eligible week shows FTMO H8/long weakness and symbol concentration. Needs cross-lane and recent-window attribution.
active_candidates.next_test: Tag by symbol, timeframe, side, session, ATR/risk regime, spread-risk, EMA/momentum/RSI/MACD-style context, and policy epoch.

active_candidates.name: Account outcome divergence
active_candidates.current_hypothesis: Strategy R and broker PnL can diverge because of symbol pip value, commission/swap, volume rounding, broker feed, and risk policy.
active_candidates.next_test: Attribute R versus broker PnL by lane, symbol, volume, pip value, commission/swap, and policy epoch.

rejected_hypotheses.name: Simple H8 low-spread-only filter
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected because it was live-weak but historically positive; do not repeat without new evidence.

data_gaps: Stable docs/live_weekly_performance.html can lag the latest ignored weekly packet; command center must label packet freshness.
Historical consistency is unavailable when bounded current-week fetch cannot prove first-live/source-start metadata.
Offline indicator tags are not yet attached to every candidate cohort; compute them in ignored analysis packets before live filters.

ownership.strategy_agent_reflection: The strategy agent owns noticing weak performance, checking whether evidence is complete, converting repeated weak cohorts into offline research candidates, rejecting unsupported hypotheses, and asking for missing data or tooling before recommending live changes. This dashboard should make that ownership visible instead of hiding it inside dense weekly tables.
role_coverage: Main Orchestrator: routes material dashboard changes through change gate.
LPFS Strategy Improvement Agent: owns candidate questions, interpretation, and next action.
Documentation and Workflow Agent: owns source-of-truth clarity and generated-page hygiene.
Repo Auditor: checks stale claims and generated artifact scope.
Reliability Reviewer: reviews live-status wording and operational boundaries.
Independent Issue Verifier: verifies issue/data truth and production-impact claims.
QA/Test Engineer: owns command-center tests and link/contract coverage.
Risk Manager: checks risk framing and no-live-change boundaries.
Strategy Trader: checks trader-facing interpretation and candidate wording.
Data Engineer: checks evidence lineage, hashes, and data-quality fields.
Dashboard/UI owner: proposed lightweight role for first-screen hierarchy, caveats, readability, and refresh workflow.
maintenance_checklist: Update source config first, then regenerate HTML.
Show freshness, packet path, and hash for every current claim.
Never present incomplete evidence as valid performance.
Keep candidate status separate from approved live strategy changes.
Run dashboard tests and process audit before publishing.
